case_id: Eramba GRC system - Compliance Finding escalation task

pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 35 creates compliance_analysis record with this exact name; export_result.sh line 59 queries for this finding by name
Code analysis: export_result.sh line 31 queries risks table for this exact title using LIKE 'Risk: Legacy Payment Server Vulnerabilities'

Code analysis: task.json description (line 5) specifies risk should include 'EOL operating system'; setup_task.sh (line 35) creates finding with 'Server is EOL and cannot be patched'; verifier.py (lines 90-91) checks for keywords 'eol', 'patch', 'operating system'

pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic data created by setup_task.sh SQL INSERT statements
pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh lines 24-36 show INSERT statements that create compliance_analysis record with seeded data; no external data source is referenced

pi_items.verified_value: Eramba is an open-source GRC (Governance, Risk & Compliance) platform with databases for managing risks, compliance findings, policies, and security controls
pi_items.source: Web research: Eramba documentation and GitHub repository confirm it is open-source GRC software. Docker compose (config/docker-compose.yml line 35) uses image ghcr.io/eramba/eramba:latest - the official Eramba container

Code analysis: setup_task.sh creates records in compliance_packages (line 26) and compliance_analysis (line 34); export_result.sh queries risks table (line 28) and checks join table compliance_analysis_risks (line 65-67) for risk-finding linkage

privileged_info_summary: The task escalates a synthetic compliance finding into Eramba's Risk Register. The compliance finding is named 'Unpatched Legacy Payment Server' and is created by setup_task.sh through SQL INSERT into the compliance_analysis table. The agent must create a new Risk titled 'Risk: Legacy Payment Server Vulnerabilities' with a description containing the keyword 'EOL operating system' and link it to the existing compliance finding. Eramba is an open-source GRC platform deployed via Docker. All data is synthetic (seeded via SQL scripts) and not sourced from external systems.
